title: Inline small CFuncs to reduce function call overhead
body:
Resolves #2367

## Summary

Add `--inline-cfuncs` and `--inline-cfuncs-product` options to inline small CFunc calls directly into their callers. This reduces function call overhead when `--output-split-cfuncs` places functions in separate compilation units that the C++ compiler cannot inline.

**Two thresholds:**
- `--inline-cfuncs <n>` (default 20): Always inline if function has ≤ n nodes
- `--inline-cfuncs-product <n>` (default 200): Also inline if size × call_count ≤ n

**Functions are inlined when they:**
- Meet size thresholds above
- Have no `$c()` statements
- Have void return type
- Are in the same scope as caller

**Local variables** in inlined functions are cloned with unique names (`__Vinline_<func>_<var>`).

Adds V3Stats tracking: `"Optimizations, Inlined CFuncs"`

This optimization is automatically disabled when `--prof-cfuncs` or `--trace` is used.

## Changes from previous PR #6765

Based on maintainer feedback:
- Moved from V3OrderCFuncEmitter to separate V3InlineCFuncs pass (runs after V3Reloop)
- Changed `--inline-cfuncs` from boolean to threshold option
- Added second tunable `--inline-cfuncs-product` for size×calls threshold
- Implemented variable merging (local variables now supported)
- Added V3Stats tracking
- No multithreading (single-threaded pass)

## Updates (v3)

Addressed file-specific review comments:
- `t_opt_inline_cfuncs.py`: Use exact count instead of matching any
- `t_timing_debug1.py`: Use `--binary` instead of `--exe --main --timing`
- `t_verilated_debug.py`: Revert to empty flags to allow inlining

## Updates (v4)

Addressed file-specific review comments:
- `t_opt_inline_cfuncs_threshold.py`: Use `--binary` and exact count (0)
- `t_opt_inline_cfuncs_threshold.v`: Simplify if/else to use `$stop`
- `t_timing_debug1.out`: Restored from master
- `t_timing_debug1.py`: Added `-CFLAGS -DVL_DEBUG` to ensure debug output
- `V3InlineCFuncs.cpp`: Use `VNVisitorConst` for better performance
- `V3InlineCFuncs.cpp`: Add `!entryPoint()` check to `isInlineable()`

## Updates (v5)

- `t_opt_inline_cfuncs_threshold.py`: Use product threshold 0 to guarantee count of 0 regardless of test file contents

## Test plan

- [x] `make test` passes locally
- [x] CI passes on fork
- [x] `t_opt_inline_cfuncs.py` - verifies inlining happens
- [x] `t_opt_inline_cfuncs_off.py` - verifies `--inline-cfuncs 0` disables
- [x] `t_opt_inline_cfuncs_threshold.py` - tests threshold behavior (branch coverage)
- [x] Documentation added to `docs/guide/exe_verilator.rst`
- [x] ARGUMENT SUMMARY updated in `bin/verilator`
